name: Riverfront Observation Tower
description: Glass-floor observation deck with a full circle view of the Rockies on a clear day.

name: Prairie Heritage Museum
description: Four floors of regional art and settlement history with a strong Indigenous gallery.

name: Island Park Loop
description: Island park in the river with a paved loop

name: East Village Music Hall
description: Interactive music museum in a landmark building; instrument rooms on every floor.

name: Pedestrian Bridge Walk
description: Red spiral footbridge over the river — fifteen minutes
column16: and the best photo in the city.

name: Inglewood Bird Sanctuary
description: Riverside wetland trails with roughly 270 recorded bird species.

name: Heritage Village Railway
description: Living-history village with a working steam train and costumed interpreters.

name: Calgary Zoo Gardens
description: Large zoo and botanical garden on an island site; the penguin house is the draw.

name: Kensington Shopping Walk
description: Compact independent retail strip that pairs well with a coffee stop.

name: Stephen Avenue Mall
description: Pedestrian-only sandstone street with buskers through the lunch hour.

name: Glenmore Reservoir Path
description: Lakeside path with sailing club views and long uninterrupted stretches.

name: Fish Creek Valley Trail
description: One of the largest urban parks in the country; deer sightings are routine.

name: Contemporary Art Gallery
description: Rotating contemporary exhibitions in a converted power station.

name: Fort Calgary Historic Site
description: Interpretive centre at the confluence of the two rivers where the city began.

name: Beltline Mural Route
description: Self-guided route past three dozen large-format murals; free and always open.

name: Science Discovery Centre
description: Hands-on science exhibits and a dome theatre; busy with school groups midweek.

name: Olympic Park Summer Slide
description: Former Olympic site running summer luge

name: Chinatown Cultural Centre
description: Domed cultural centre with a small museum and a good gift shop.

name: Riverwalk Promenade
description: Paved riverside promenade linking the east side back to downtown.

name: Central Library Atrium
description: Architecturally striking public library; the wood atrium is worth the detour.

name: Bow River Kayak Rental
description: Guided float trips and hourly rentals from the riverbank; summer only.

name: Military Heritage Museum
description: Regimental collections and restored vehicles in a quiet suburban site.

name: Inglewood Antique Row
description: Six blocks of antique dealers

name: Sunridge Farmers Market
description: Weekend producer market with prepared food stalls and live music.

name: Rotary Park Lookout
description: Small hilltop park with the classic skyline view back across the river.
